title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Quantity Clinic: Repeated Percent
summary: Audit prices, discounts, taxes, tips, population changes, and performance claims. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg repeated percent increment in a consumer percent-claim audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model repeated percent change with compounding rather than repeated addition.
Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Repeated Percent evidence
steps.instruction: A price of $110 is reduced by 16%. What is the new price?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use 110 × (1 − 16/100), then compare the result with the original price.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use 110 × (1 − 16/100), then compare the result with the original price.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Defend the Repeated Percent model

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
